issues: The headline is AI-invented and does not appear in the OCR. No clear bold headline is present in the OCR text — the article begins with body copy.

The series is described as the 'Alberta-B.C. junior hockey playoff series' or 'western Centennial Cup tier two junior hockey semifinal' — not 'Centennial Cup tier two semifinal' without context, but that detail is fine. More importantly, the draft does not mention the goalscorers named in the OCR: Dave Tomlinson (3 goals), Rob Clarke, Mike Claringbull, Jason Phillips, and Jim Gunn.
The draft attributes Orland Kurtenbach solely as 'head coach' but the OCR quotes him as 'general manager-coach.'
The draft omits the key context that Red Deer had won the Alberta championship by upsetting Calgary Canucks and St. Albert Saints, and that Jamie Stewart was the Sockeyes goaltender.

The draft short description says 'game seven of Centennial Cup tier two semifinal' which is accurate but omits that this is an Abbott Cup / western Centennial Cup tier two path — minor but acceptable. However the short description is otherwise accurate.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: The Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Red Deer Rustlers 9-1 in game seven of the western Centennial Cup tier-two junior hockey semifinal series on April 17, 1987, with Dave Tomlinson scoring three goals.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes eliminated the Red Deer Rustlers 9-1 in the seventh and deciding game of the western Centennial Cup tier-two junior hockey semifinal on April 17, 1987, at the Red Deer Arena. An exhausted Rustlers squad, playing their 27th post-season game in 47 days, could not match the bigger, more talented Sockeyes, who blew open a scoreless game with five unanswered second-period goals. Dave Tomlinson led the Sockeyes with three goals, while Rob Clarke, Mike Claringbull, Jason Phillips, and Jim Gunn also scored; Jamie Stewart was in goal for Richmond. Pete deGraaf scored the lone Red Deer goal late in the third period, and Sockeyes general manager-coach Orland Kurtenbach praised his team's effort after the series win.
review.notes: No headline is visible in the OCR — the article opens directly with body copy. The AI-invented headline has been cleared. The article is a Red Deer Advocate game recap/column covering the Sockeyes' series-clinching win and is highly relevant to the archive. Note the OCR appears to be cut off at the end mid-sentence; additional scorers may be listed on a continuation not captured.
